forecasts.reasoning: The Artemis Accords have witnessed a strong and consistent pace of new signatories, growing from 33 at the end of 2023 to 55 by mid-May 2025 (averaging over 1 signatory per month). Historically, signings tend to cluster around major space events and state visits. The 68th session of the UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS) is scheduled to begin on June 25, 2025, which often acts as a catalyst for diplomatic space announcements. Furthermore, following an international Artemis Accords workshop held in the UAE on May 21-22, 2025, NASA explicitly stated that it "anticipates additional countries will sign in the coming weeks and months." While signings are somewhat clustered (meaning not every single month sees activity), this strong recent official signaling, combined with an empirical long-term base rate of roughly 50-60% of months having at least one signing, pushes the probability for June 2025 slightly above the baseline. The risk of missing the window lies mainly in signatures being delayed to early July during the latter half of the COPUOS session.

Despite recent critical media profiles and calls from progressive groups for him to resign due to behavioral and health concerns, Senator John Fetterman has adamantly stated that he will not resign. Furthermore, he has repeatedly quashed rumors of leaving the Democratic Party. The resolution date of July 1, 2025, provides a very short window (roughly one month from the May 29, 2025 knowledge cutoff) for such a drastic event to occur. Even if Fetterman were to drop his Democratic registration to become an Independent in the near future, precedent (e.g., Sinema, Manchin) suggests he would likely continue caucusing with the Democrats to maintain committee assignments. Therefore, the chance of him resigning, switching to the Republican caucus, being expelled, or passing away in the next 33 days is extremely low.
